market.summary: AI inference cost-routing and AI gateway software for production teams that need the cheapest model or GPU mix that still meets latency, quality, and compliance SLAs.
market.buyer: CTO or head of AI platform
market.whyNow: Frontier-AI capital and state-backed compute investment are making inference efficiency a first-order budget issue, while production teams want dynamic routing instead of static model choices.
market.wedge: Start with per-request routing and spend tracking, then expand into policy, observability, fallback, and optimization loops.
risks: Multi-model integration adds switching costs but also complexity

competitors.name: LiteLLM
competitors.positioning: AI gateway for model access, fallbacks, spend tracking, and load balancing across 100+ LLMs.

competitors.name: OpenRouter
competitors.positioning: Unified LLM interface with multi-provider access and pricing-aware routing.

competitors.name: Portkey
competitors.positioning: AI gateway plus observability, guardrails, governance, and prompt management.

competitors.name: Helicone
competitors.positioning: Routing, debugging, and analytics layer for AI applications.

competitors.name: AWS Bedrock / cloud-native gateways
competitors.positioning: Incumbent platform features can bundle routing, procurement, and deployment into existing cloud spend.

sources.title: Google to invest up to $40 billion in AI rival Anthropic
sources.note: Signals strategic frontier-AI capital and rising inference demand.

sources.title: South Korea to invest $166 million in AI chip startup Rebellions
sources.note: Shows public money flowing into domestic compute capacity.

sources.title: OpenRouter homepage
sources.note: Direct competitor for unified model access and routing.

sources.title: LiteLLM homepage
sources.note: Direct competitor for AI gateway, fallbacks, and spend tracking.

sources.note: Direct competitor for gateway, observability, and governance.
